overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate capped at 10% per annum (vs. 18% in standard agreement)
overrides.why_it_matters: Reduces late payment penalty exposure for California franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete might not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be able to open competing breakfast restaurants after leaving the system

overrides.override_summary: Oregon governing law provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California courts may apply California law rather than Oregon law to disputes

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable under California Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: The lost revenue damages provision (NPV of 5 years of fees) may be challenged in California

overrides.override_summary: Corporations Code §31512 and §31512.1 restrict effectiveness of general releases and disclaimers. Any provision disclaiming franchisor representations, reliance on FDD, or violations of franchise law is void and unenforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees retain broader legal protections upon renewal and transfer despite release requirements

overrides.override_summary: California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000-20043 provide rights concerning termination, transfer, or nonrenewal that override inconsistent agreement provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees have statutory protections against arbitrary termination

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law applies (replacing Oregon), except for Federal Arbitration Act and other federal laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees benefit from Illinois franchise law protections rather than Oregon law

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction and venue outside Illinois is void. However, arbitration may take place outside Illinois.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in Oregon courts, though arbitration in Oregon remains possible

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee rights upon termination and non-renewal governed by Sections 19 and 20 of the Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees have statutory protections that override contract termination provisions

overrides.override_summary: Any condition purporting to waive compliance with IFDA or any other Illinois law is void (Section 41)
overrides.why_it_matters: General releases required at renewal/transfer cannot waive rights under Illinois law

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation site must be in Washington State (or mutually agreed location) if franchise purchased in WA. Litigation may be brought in Washington.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchisees are not required to travel to Oregon for dispute resolution

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act (FIPA) prevails in any conflict with the agreement. RCW 19.100.180 may supersede agreement regarding termination and renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchise law provides potentially stronger protections than Oregon law

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void and unenforceable against employees earning <$100,000/year (adjusted for inflation) and independent contractors earning <$250,000/year. No-poach provisions entirely void.
overrides.why_it_matters: Most restaurant employees would be below the $100K threshold, effectively eliminating employee noncompetes. No-poach restrictions eliminated entirely.

overrides.override_summary: Release or waiver may not include rights under the FIPA except when executed pursuant to a negotiated settlement with independent counsel after agreement is in effect
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard general releases at renewal/transfer cannot waive WA franchise law rights

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to the extent they reflect reasonable estimated or actual costs in effecting a transfer
overrides.why_it_matters: The transfer fee of 50% of then-current IFF or greater of 3%/$20K may be challenged if it exceeds actual costs

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede agreement regarding termination and renewal. Court decisions may also supersede.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington law provides additional termination protections beyond what the agreement allows
